Topic: 19.1 Occurrence, Preparation, and Properties of Transition Metals and Their Compounds
Question: Which of the following elements is most likely to be used to prepare La by the reduction of La_2O_3: Al, C, or Fe? Why?
Answer: Al is used because it is the strongest reducing agent and the only option listed that can provide sufficient driving force to convert La(III) into La.

Question: Which of the following elements is most likely to form an oxide with the formula MO_3: Zr, Nb, or Mo?
Answer: Mo

Topic: 19.1 Occurrence, Preparation, and Properties of Transition Metals and Their Compounds
Question: The following reactions all occur in a blast furnace. Which of these are redox reactions? (a) $$\text{3Fe}_{2} \text{O}_{3} ( s ) + \text{CO} ( g ) ⟶ \text{2Fe}_{3} \text{O}_{4} ( s ) + \text{CO}_{2} ( g )$$ (b) $$\text{Fe}_{3} \text{O}_{4} ( s ) + \text{CO} ( g ) ⟶ \text{3FeO} ( s ) + \text{CO}_{2} ( g )$$ (c) $$\text{FeO} ( s ) + \text{CO} ( g ) ⟶ \text{Fe} ( l ) + \text{CO}_{2} ( g )$$ (d) $$\text{C} ( s ) + \text{O}_{2} ( g ) ⟶ \text{CO}_{2} ( g )$$ (e) $$\text{C} ( s ) + \text{CO}_{2} ( g ) ⟶ \text{2CO} ( g )$$ (f) $$\text{CaCO}_{3} ( s ) ⟶ \text{CaO} ( s ) + \text{CO}_{2} ( g )$$ (g) $$\text{CaO} ( s ) + \text{SiO}_{2} ( s ) ⟶ \text{CaSiO}_{3} ( l )$$

Topic: 19.1 Occurrence, Preparation, and Properties of Transition Metals and Their Compounds
Question: Why is the formation of slag useful during the smelting of iron?
Answer: The CaSiO_3 slag is less dense than the molten iron, so it can easily be separated. Also, the floating slag layer creates a barrier that prevents the molten iron from exposure to O_2, which would oxidize the Fe back to Fe_2O_3.

Topic: 19.1 Occurrence, Preparation, and Properties of Transition Metals and Their Compounds
Question: The standard reduction potential for the reaction $$[ \text{Co} ( \text{H}_{2} \text{O} )_{6} ]^{3+} ( a q ) + \text{e}^{-} ⟶ [ {\text{Co} ( \text{H}}_{2} \text{O} )_{6} ]^{2+} ( a q )$$ is about 1.8 V. The reduction potential for the reaction $$[ \text{Co} ( \text{NH}_{3} )_{6} ]^{3+} ( a q ) + \text{e}^{-} ⟶ [ \text{Co} ( \text{NH}_{3} )_{6} ]^{2+} ( a q )$$ is +0.1 V. Calculate the cell potentials to show whether the complex ions, [Co(H_2O)_6]^2+ and/or [Co(NH_3)_6]^2+, can be oxidized to the corresponding cobalt(III) complex by oxygen.
Answer: E ° = -0.6 V, E ° is negative so this reduction is not spontaneous. E ° = +1.1 V

Topic: 19.1 Occurrence, Preparation, and Properties of Transition Metals and Their Compounds
Question: Predict the products of each of the following reactions. (Note: In addition to using the information in this chapter, also use the knowledge you have accumulated at this stage of your study, including information on the prediction of reaction products.) (a) $$\text{Fe} ( s ) + \text{H}_{2} \text{SO}_{4} ( a q ) ⟶$$ (b) $$\text{FeCl}_{3} ( a q ) + \text{NaOH} ( a q ) ⟶$$ (c) $$\text{Mn} ( \text{OH} )_{2} ( s ) + \text{HBr} ( a q ) ⟶$$ (d) $$\text{Cr} ( s ) + \text{O}_{2} ( g ) ⟶$$ (e) $$\text{Mn}_{2} \text{O}_{3} ( s ) + \text{HCl} ( a q ) ⟶$$ (f) $$\text{Ti} ( s ) + x s \text{F}_{2} ( g ) ⟶$$
Answer: (a) $$\text{Fe} ( s ) + 2 \text{H}_{3} \text{O}^{\text{+}} ( a q ) + \text{SO}_{4} ^{2-} ( a q ) ⟶ \text{Fe}^{2+} ( a q ) + \text{SO}_{4} ^{2-} ( a q ) + \text{H}_{2} ( g ) + 2 \text{H}_{2} \text{O} ( l ) ;$$ (b) $$\text{FeCl}_{3} ( a q ) + \text{3Na}^{\text{+}} ( a q ) + \text{3OH}^{\text{unicode[:-]}} ( a q ) ⟶ \text{Fe} ( \text{OH} )_{3} ( s ) + \text{3Na}^{\text{+}} ( a q ) + \text{3Cl}^{\text{+}} ( a q ) ;$$ (c) $$\text{Mn} ( \text{OH} )_{2} ( s ) + 2 \text{H}_{3} \text{O}^{\text{+}} ( a q ) + \text{2Br}^{\text{unicode[:-]}} ( a q ) ⟶ \text{Mn}^{2+} ( a q ) + \text{2Br}^{\text{unicode[:-]}} ( a q ) + 4 \text{H}_{2} \text{O} ( l ) ;$$ (d) $$\text{4Cr} ( s ) + 3 \text{O}_{2} ( g ) ⟶ 2 \text{Cr}_{2} \text{O}_{3} ( s ) ;$$ (e) $$\text{Mn}_{2} \text{O}_{3} ( s ) + 6 \text{H}_{3} \text{O}^{\text{+}} ( a q ) + \text{6Cl}^{\text{unicode[:-]}} ( a q ) ⟶ 2 \text{MnCl}_{3} ( s ) + 9 \text{H}_{2} \text{O} ( l ) ;$$ (f) $$\text{Ti} ( s ) + x s \text{F}_{2} ( g ) ⟶ \text{TiF}_{4} ( g )$$

Topic: 19.1 Occurrence, Preparation, and Properties of Transition Metals and Their Compounds
Question: Describe the electrolytic process for refining copper.

Topic: 19.1 Occurrence, Preparation, and Properties of Transition Metals and Their Compounds
Question: Predict the products of the following reactions and balance the equations. (a) Zn is added to a solution of Cr_2(SO_4)_3 in acid. (b) FeCl_2 is added to a solution containing an excess of $$\text{Cr}_{2} \text{O}_{7} ^{2-}$$ in hydrochloric acid. (c) Cr^2+ is added to $$\text{Cr}_{2} \text{O}_{7} ^{2-}$$ in acid solution. (d) Mn is heated with CrO_3. (e) CrO is added to 2HNO_3 in water. (f) FeCl_3 is added to an aqueous solution of NaOH.
Answer: (a) $$\text{Cr}_{2} ( \text{SO}_{4} )_{3} ( a q ) + \text{2Zn} ( s ) + \text{2H}_{3} \text{O}^{\text{+}} ( a q ) ⟶ \text{2Zn}^{2+} ( a q ) + \text{H}_{2} ( g ) + \text{2H}_{2} \text{O} ( l ) + \text{2Cr}^{2+} ( a q ) + \text{3SO}_{4} ^{2-} ( a q ) ;$$ (b) $$\text{4TiCl}_{3} ( s ) + \text{CrO}_{4} ^{2-} ( a q ) + \text{8H}^{\text{+}} ( a q ) ⟶ \text{4Ti}^{4+} ( a q ) + \text{Cr} ( s ) + \text{4H}_{2} \text{O} ( l ) + \text{12Cl}^{\text{unicode[:-]}} ( a q ) ;$$ (c) In acid solution between pH 2 and pH 6, $$\text{CrO}_{4} ^{2-}$$ forms $$\text{HCrO}_{4} ^{\text{unicode[:-]}} ,$$ which is in equilibrium with dichromate ion. The reaction is $$\text{2HCrO}_{4} ^{\text{unicode[:-]}} ( a q ) ⟶ \text{Cr}_{2} \text{O}_{7} ^{2-} ( a q ) + \text{H}_{2} \text{O} ( l ) .$$ At other acidic pHs, the reaction is $$\text{3Cr}^{2+} ( a q ) + \text{CrO}_{4} ^{2-} ( a q ) + \text{8H}_{3} \text{O}^{\text{+}} ( a q ) ⟶ \text{4Cr}^{3+} ( a q ) + \text{12H}_{2} \text{O} ( l ) ;$$ (d) $$\left(\text{8CrO}\right)_{3} \left(\right. s \left.\right) + \text{9Mn} \left(\right. s \left.\right) \overset{\Delta}{\rightarrow} \left(\text{4Cr}\right)_{2} \left(\text{O}\right)_{3} \left(\right. s \left.\right) + \left(\text{3Mn}\right)_{3} \left(\text{O}\right)_{4} \left(\right. s \left.\right) ;$$ (e) $$\text{CrO} ( s ) + \text{2H}_{3} \text{O}^{\text{+}} ( a q ) + \text{2NO}_{3} ^{\text{unicode[:-]}} ( a q ) ⟶ \text{Cr}^{2+} ( a q ) + \text{2NO}_{3} ^{\text{unicode[:-]}} ( a q ) + \text{3H}_{2} \text{O} ( l ) ;$$ (f) $$\text{CrCl}_{3} ( s ) + \text{3NaOH} ( a q ) ⟶ \text{Cr} ( \text{OH} )_{3} ( s ) + \text{3Na}^{\text{+}} ( a q ) + \text{3Cl}^{\text{unicode[:-]}} ( a q )$$

Topic: 19.1 Occurrence, Preparation, and Properties of Transition Metals and Their Compounds
Question: What is the gas produced when iron(II) sulfide is treated with a nonoxidizing acid?

Topic: 19.1 Occurrence, Preparation, and Properties of Transition Metals and Their Compounds
Question: Predict the products of each of the following reactions and then balance the chemical equations. (a) Fe is heated in an atmosphere of steam. (b) NaOH is added to a solution of Fe(NO_3)_3. (c) FeSO_4 is added to an acidic solution of KMnO_4. (d) Fe is added to a dilute solution of H_2SO_4. (e) A solution of Fe(NO_3)_2 and HNO_3 is allowed to stand in air. (f) FeCO_3 is added to a solution of HClO_4. (g) Fe is heated in air.
Answer: (a) $$\text{3Fe} ( s ) + \text{4H}_{2} \text{O} ( g ) ⟶ \text{Fe}_{3} \text{O}_{4} ( s ) + \text{4H}_{2} ( g ) ;$$ (b) $$\text{3NaOH} ( a q ) + \text{Fe} ( \text{NO}_{3} )_{3} ( a q ) \overset{ \left ( \text{H}_{2} \text{O} \right ) }{ \left ( \vec{} \right ) } \text{Fe} ( \text{OH} )_{3} ( s ) + \text{3Na}^{\text{+}} ( a q ) + 3 \text{NO}_{3} ^{\text{unicode[:-]}} ( a q ) ;$$ (c) $$\text{MnO} ^{4-} + 5 \text{Fe}^{\text{2+}} + 8 \text{H}^{\text{+}} ⟶ \text{Mn} ^{\text{2+}} + 5 \text{Fe}_{3} + 4 \text{H}_{2} \text{O} ;$$ (d) $$\text{Fe} ( s ) + \text{2H}_{3} \text{O}^{\text{+}} ( a q ) + \text{SO}_{4} ^{2-} ( a q ) ⟶ \text{Fe}^{2+} ( a q ) + \text{SO}_{4} ^{2-} ( a q ) + \text{H}_{2} ( g ) + \text{2H}_{2} \text{O} ( l ) ;$$ (e) $$\text{4Fe}^{2+} ( a q ) + \text{O}_{2} ( g ) + \text{4HNO}_{3} ( a q ) ⟶ \text{4Fe}^{3+} ( a q ) + \text{2H}_{2} \text{O} ( l ) + \text{4NO}_{3} ^{\text{unicode[:-]}} ( a q ) ;$$ (f) $$\text{FeCO}_{3} ( s ) + \text{2HClO}_{4} ( a q ) ⟶ \text{Fe} ( \text{ClO}_{4} )_{2} ( a q ) + \text{H}_{2} \text{O} ( l ) + \text{CO}_{2} ( g ) ;$$ (g) $$\text{3Fe} \left(\right. s \left.\right) + \left(\text{2O}\right)_{2} \left(\right. g \left.\right) \overset{ \Delta }{\rightarrow} \left(\text{Fe}\right)_{3} \left(\text{O}\right)_{4} \left(\right. s \left.\right)$$

Topic: 19.1 Occurrence, Preparation, and Properties of Transition Metals and Their Compounds
Question: Balance the following equations by oxidation-reduction methods; note that three elements change oxidation state. $$\text{Co} ( \text{NO}_{3} )_{2} ( s ) ⟶ \text{Co}_{2} \text{O}_{3} ( s ) + \text{NO}_{2} ( g ) + \text{O}_{2} ( g )$$

Topic: 19.1 Occurrence, Preparation, and Properties of Transition Metals and Their Compounds
Question: Dilute sodium cyanide solution is slowly dripped into a slowly stirred silver nitrate solution. A white precipitate forms temporarily but dissolves as the addition of sodium cyanide continues. Use chemical equations to explain this observation. Silver cyanide is similar to silver chloride in its solubility.
Answer: As CN^- is added, $$\text{Ag}^{\text{+}} ( a q ) + \text{CN}^{\text{unicode[:-]}} ( a q ) ⟶ \text{AgCN} ( s )$$ As more CN^- is added, $$\left(\text{Ag}\right)^{+} \left(\right. a q \left.\right) + 2 \left(\text{CN}\right)^{-} \left(\right. a q \left.\right) \rightarrow \left(\left[\right. \text{Ag} \left((\text{CN})\right)_{2} \left]\right.\right)^{-} \left(\right. a q \left.\right) \\ \text{AgCN} \left(\right. s \left.\right) + \left(\text{CN}\right)^{-} \left(\right. a q \left.\right) \rightarrow \left(\left[\right. \text{Ag} \left((\text{CN})\right)_{2} \left]\right.\right)^{-} \left(\right. a q \left.\right)$$

Topic: 19.2 Coordination Chemistry of Transition Metals
Question: Specify whether the following complexes have isomers. (a) tetrahedral [Ni(CO)_2(Cl)_2] (b) trigonal bipyramidal [Mn(CO)_4NO] (c) [Pt(en)_2Cl_2]Cl_2
Answer: (a) none; (b) none; (c) The two Cl ligands can be cis or trans . When they are cis , there will also be an optical isomer.

Question: The solid anhydrous solid CoCl_2 is blue in color. Because it readily absorbs water from the air, it is used as a humidity indicator to monitor if equipment (such as a cell phone) has been exposed to excessive levels of moisture. Predict what product is formed by this reaction, and how many unpaired electrons this complex will have.
Answer: [Co(H_2O)_6]Cl_2 with three unpaired electrons.

This blue compound does not have any isomeric forms. What are the geometry and molecular formula of the blue compound?

Topic: 19.3 Spectroscopic and Magnetic Properties of Coordination Compounds
Question: Would you expect the complex [Co(en)_3]Cl_3 to have any unpaired electrons? Any isomers?
Answer: The complex does not have any unpaired electrons. The complex does not have any geometric isomers, but the mirror image is nonsuperimposable, so it has an optical isomer.

Topic: 19.3 Spectroscopic and Magnetic Properties of Coordination Compounds
Question: Would you expect the Mg_3[Cr(CN)_6]_2 to be diamagnetic or paramagnetic? Explain your reasoning.

Topic: 19.3 Spectroscopic and Magnetic Properties of Coordination Compounds
Question: Would you expect salts of the gold(I) ion, Au^+, to be colored? Explain.
Answer: No. Au^+ has a complete 5 d sublevel.

Topic: 19.3 Spectroscopic and Magnetic Properties of Coordination Compounds
Question: [CuCl_4]^2- is green. [Cu(H_2O)_6]^2+is blue. Which absorbs higher-energy photons? Which is predicted to have a larger crystal field splitting?
